bio: Some bands spend 20 years climbing the ladder. Avondale spent 20 years taking the scenic route.

After playing together in a band in high school, the members of Avondale disappeared into adulthood just long enough to collect careers, responsibilities, and a few creaks when they stand up. Now in their mid-30s, the boys have reunited under the completely reasonable assumption that the world simply wasn't ready back then for their brand of brash, but heartfelt, pop punk.

Now split between Chicago and Nashville, Avondale has become experts in the fine art of long-distance band relationships, airline rewards programs, and pretending the algorithm is the most dysfunctional member of the band. Their social feeds are filled with the kind of self-aware chaos required to survive being a modern rock band, because apparently making great music is only half the battle now.

Avondale's debut full-length, Holding Patterns, was recorded with producer Seth Henderson (Knuckle Puck, State Champs, Broadside, and more) at Always Be Genius Studios in St. Augustine, Florida and arrives on July 31st, 2026. The band is widely considered to be on the verge of a mainstream breakthrough - mostly by themselves.

Once the band gets off their asses and starts making the trek between Chicago and Nashville more often, live shows will be posted. Until then, Avondale will continue acting like they've already made it. And honestly? That kind of confidence is contagious.
You can pre-order Holding Patterns now!
